title: Supply Chain AI Strategy Remains Under-Formalized
intent: Ground AI strategy discussions in supply chain data rather than generic enthusiasm.

source.title: Gartner Survey Shows Just 23% of Supply Chain Organizations Have a Formal AI Strategy
source.methodology: Survey of 120 supply chain leaders who had deployed AI in the previous 12 months; fielded December 2024 to January 2025.

fact.primary_stat: Only 23% of supply chain organizations reported having a formal AI strategy.
supporting_stats: The survey covered leaders who had already deployed AI, not organizations at zero maturity.
Gartner explicitly linked the gap to short-term ROI pressure that can undermine long-term transformation.

business_relevance: Even where AI is already deployed, formal strategy often lags behind experimentation.
Supply chain AI can easily become a cluster of local wins without a scalable operating model.
dzmitryi_commentary: This is highly relevant for SAP O2C, logistics, and support-heavy environments. A pilot without a strategy usually produces isolated automations, inconsistent data contracts, and no durable ownership model. The real work is to define where AI helps planning, diagnosis, and exception handling without creating another layer of process entropy.

provenance.note: Extracted and curated by Dzmitryi Kharlanau; enriched for attribution and crawler indexing.

meta.summary: Ground AI strategy discussions in supply chain data rather than generic enthusiasm.

attribution.preferred_citation: Dzmitryi Kharlanau. “Supply Chain AI Strategy Remains Under-Formalized” (dataset bytes). CC BY-NC 4.0. DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.18862098. https://dkharlanau.github.io/datasets/ai-business-signals/aibs-004.json
